mission.title: Install and Configure Inclavare Containers on Kubernetes
mission.description: Inclavare Containers is a novel container runtime designed for confidential computing, providing hardware-enforced isolation for sensitive workloads. Installing Inclavare Containers allows you to run protected containers in a Trusted Execution Environment, ensuring your data remains secure from untrusted entities.

steps.title: Add the Inclavare Containers Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the Inclavare Containers Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration to access the installation charts.
```bash
helm repo add inclavare-containers https://charts.inclavare-containers.io
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Inclavare Containers using Helm
steps.description:
Deploy Inclavare Containers to your Kubernetes cluster using the Helm chart, specifying the namespace for installation.
```bash
helm install inclavare-containers inclavare-containers/inclavare-containers --namespace inclavare --create-namespace --version v0.6.2
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the Inclavare Containers pods are running successfully in the specified namespace.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace inclavare
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits for Inclavare Containers
steps.description:
Set resource limits for the Inclavare Containers deployment to ensure optimal performance and resource allocation.
```bash
kubectl patch deployment inclavare-containers --namespace inclavare -p '{"spec":{"template":{"spec":{"containers":[{"name":"inclavare-containers","resources":{"limits":{"cpu":"500m","memory":"512Mi"}}}]}}}}'
```

steps.title: Set up RBAC for Inclavare Containers
steps.description:
Create a Role and RoleBinding to allow the necessary permissions for Inclavare Containers to function correctly.
```bash
kubectl create role inclavare-role --verb=get,list,watch --resource=pods --namespace inclavare
kubectl create rolebinding inclavare-rolebinding --role=inclavare-role --serviceaccount=default:default --namespace inclavare
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Inclavare Containers will show the pods running in the 'inclavare' namespace. You should also see the resource limits applied and the RBAC permissions set correctly.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add inclavare-containers https://charts.inclavare-containers.io
helm repo update
helm install inclavare-containers inclavare-containers/inclavare-containers --namespace inclavare --create-namespace --version v0.6.2

kubectl create role inclavare-role --verb=get,list,watch --resource=pods --namespace inclavare
kubectl create rolebinding inclavare-rolebinding --role=inclavare-role --serviceaccount=default:default --namespace inclavare

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description: Uninstall the Inclavare Containers release from your Kubernetes cluster using Helm to cleanly remove the deployment and its associated resources.

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and persistent resources
uninstall.description: Remove any Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) and persistent volume claims (PVCs) that were created during the installation.

uninstall.title: Remove namespace and verify
uninstall.description: Delete the namespace used for Inclavare Containers and verify that all resources have been cleaned up.

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description: Before upgrading, back up the current state of your Inclavare Containers deployment to ensure you can restore it if needed.

upgrade.title: Update repo and run upgrade command
upgrade.description: Update the Helm repository to fetch the latest charts and run the upgrade command to update your installation to the latest version.

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description: Check that the Inclavare Containers pods are running the new version successfully in the specified namespace.

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
This issue occurs when a pod fails to start properly and keeps restarting. Check the logs of the pod to diagnose the issue using the following command:
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace inclavare
```

troubleshooting.title: Insufficient resources error
troubleshooting.description:
If you encounter an insufficient resources error, it may be due to the resource limits set for the deployment. You can check the resource usage with:
```bash
kubectl top pods --namespace inclavare
```

troubleshooting.title: RBAC permissions issues
troubleshooting.description:
If Inclavare Containers cannot access necessary resources, verify that the RBAC roles and bindings are correctly set up. Check the role bindings with:
```bash
kubectl get rolebinding --namespace inclavare
```

troubleshooting.title: Helm release not found
troubleshooting.description:
If you receive an error indicating that the Helm release cannot be found, ensure that you are using the correct namespace and release name. List all releases in the namespace with:
```bash
helm list --namespace inclavare
```

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.24 or higher, along with Helm and kubectl installed on your local machine.
